description:
You are an IT-support technician who just received a ticket complaining that the “latest” log link is missing.  
The previous technician already copied the real log file into:

/home/user/helpdesk/logs/2023-Sept-Audit.log

but never recreated the symbolic link.

Your job:

1. Inside the same directory (/home/user/helpdesk/logs), create or overwrite a **symbolic link** named  
   latest.log  
   so that it points to the file 2023-Sept-Audit.log that is already there.

2. Immediately after creating the link, record its resolved, absolute target path in a tiny verification file named  
   /home/user/helpdesk/logs/symlink_status.txt

   • The verification file must contain **exactly one line** (ending with a newline, “\n”).  
   • That single line must be the fully-qualified absolute path to the real file, i.e.:  
     /home/user/helpdesk/logs/2023-Sept-Audit.log

Constraints & hints:

• Accomplish both steps with a single terminal command line (shell operators like “&&” or “;” are allowed, so long as everything is typed on one line).  
• Do not use sudo or change directory ownerships; everything needed is already writable by you.  
• The automated test will verify two things:  
  1. That /home/user/helpdesk/logs/latest.log exists and is a symbolic link whose target is 2023-Sept-Audit.log.  
  2. That /home/user/helpdesk/logs/symlink_status.txt exists and contains exactly the one-line absolute path shown above.

If those two checks pass, the ticket is considered resolved.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

Directory:
  /home/user/helpdesk/logs      (already exists, mode 755)

File:
  /home/user/helpdesk/logs/2023-Sept-Audit.log
      Content: (two lines)
        2023-09-01 00:00:01 Start of audit
        2023-09-01 00:05:47 Audit completed

Desired final state after the agent finishes:

1. Symbolic link:
   /home/user/helpdesk/logs/latest.log
   → must point to /home/user/helpdesk/logs/2023-Sept-Audit.log

2. Verification file:
   /home/user/helpdesk/logs/symlink_status.txt
   Contents (including terminating newline, no extra spaces):
     /home/user/helpdesk/logs/2023-Sept-Audit.log\n

   File permissions: default user-creation perms (e.g., 644).

The grading script will:
   a) readlink -f /home/user/helpdesk/logs/latest.log  # must exactly match the path above
   b) cat /home/user/helpdesk/logs/symlink_status.txt  # must output the exact same single line
